chemistry.scope_summary: Single example reaction used as benchmark to demonstrate light-limited kinetics and the effectiveness of the LSC-photomicroreactor system

optimization_table.key_findings: 200 ppm LR305 doping showed highest conversion with 4.5-fold acceleration compared to non-doped reactor

figures.caption: Figure 1.  Working principle of the LSC-PM. A) Comparison and analogy of solar harvesting and photon transfer in a leaf and in a LSC-PM. In the leaf photosystem, the energy of the photons harvested by the antenna pigment molecules is transferred to the reaction center where it eventually reaches the primary electron acceptor. Analogously, in the LSC-PM, the photons absorbed by the embedded fluores

Figure 2.  Edge emission measurements. A) Experimental setup for the measurement of edge emissions. B) Edge emissions of the LSC-PM with the channels filled with MB solutions of varying concentration, the percentage refers to the total incident. LSC-PM mediated wavelength conversion and light transport. A) LSC-PM mediated wavelength conversion. Using blue LEDs as light source, the direct excitation of methylene blue (MB) is limited as evident by the low conversions obtained with the non-doped reactor (black squares). Figure 4.  Solar light outdoor experiment. A–C) 30-minute snapshots of the performance of a 200 ppm LR305 LSC-PM and a non-doped reactor on a partly sunny summer day with scattered cloud cover at different residence times (A 20 s, B 15 s, C 10 s). The average conversion in the 30- minute timeframe is reported along with the standard deviation. The LSC-PM is not only more efficient in gathering sol

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Limited direct excitation of methylene blue with blue LEDs leads to low conversion
translation_logic.flow_advantage: LSC-photomicroreactor enables wavelength conversion and light transport resulting in 4.5-fold acceleration
translation_logic.what_changed: Implementation of luminescent solar concentrator with LR305 doping for enhanced photon collection and conversion
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same photocatalyst (methylene blue), same reaction chemistry (singlet oxygen cycloaddition)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow operation with better light utilization reduces exposure time

figure_descriptions: Working principle comparison between leaf and LSC-PM
Edge emission measurements and optimization plot showing light input vs conversion

notes: Flow-only study without direct batch comparison. Focus on LSC-photomicroreactor technology for enhanced light utilization. Key finding is 4.5-fold acceleration with 200 ppm LR305 doping compared to non-doped reactor.
